items.correct: No — humans and chimpanzees share a common ancestor; both have evolved from it.
items.misconception: Humans evolved directly from chimpanzees, which is why chimps still exist as a less-evolved version.
items.random_wrong: Humans evolved from monkeys but chimps evolved from gorillas.
items.why: The phrase 'descended from apes' and linear evolution diagrams encourage 'A turned into B' thinking, missing the branching tree model.

items.q: What does a gene code for?
items.correct: Typically a polypeptide (protein) — through transcription to mRNA and translation by ribosomes.
items.misconception: A gene codes for a specific physical feature like eye colour directly.
items.random_wrong: Each gene codes for an organ or body part as a whole.
items.why: Students learn 'gene for eye colour' shorthand, then think genes directly produce traits rather than producing proteins that contribute to traits.

items.q: What does it mean for an allele to be 'dominant'?
items.correct: It is expressed in the phenotype when at least one copy is present (in a heterozygote).
items.misconception: A dominant allele is the most common allele in a population.
items.random_wrong: A dominant allele physically destroys the recessive allele in the genome.
items.why: The everyday meaning of 'dominant' (most prevalent, strongest) misleads students into thinking it relates to frequency or power.

items.q: Which speciation type requires a geographic barrier?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Allopatric. “Sympatric” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Mule (horse × donkey) is sterile. This is an example of:
items.why: Correct answer: Postzygotic barrier. “Prezygotic barrier” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: If a tetraploid (4n) plant has 32 chromosomes, how many chromosomes were in the diploid parent (2n)?
items.why: Exact answer: 16. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
